Center: Center of Nuclear Physics Data, Russian Federal Nuclear Center (VNIIEF), Sarov, Russia
ref: Jour: Journal of the Physical Society of Japan, Vol.29, p.533 (1970)
title: The (3He,alpha) reaction on 7Li from 5.0 to 8.0 MeV

x4freetext: The (3He,alpha) reaction on 7Li from 5.0 to 8.0 MeV

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Journal of the Physical Society of Japan, Vol.29, p.533 (1970)

x4freetext: A doubly ionized 3He++
The target was made by evaporating the natural metallic
lithium onto a thin carbon foil and had a thickness
of 4.5 ug/cm2.
The solid angle of the detector was
(2.10-+0.04)*10**(-3) ster., which determined from
the Coulomb scattering of protons by silver foil of
known thickness at Ep=3.0 MeV.
A monitor detector was at 120 degr.
For the determination of the
absolute cross section, target thickness obtained by
measuring the elastic scattering of protons
(0.9-1.3) MeV at the 160 degr. at lab. system and
comparing these yield with the elastic scattering data
of Warters (see MONIT-REF)
The uncertainty in the absolute cross section
statistical error
In the text (p.534) the authors gave the value
(2.49+-0.37)mb/sr for the cross section of the
7Li(He3,alpha0)6Li reaction at 160 degr. and 6 MeV
bombarding energy. But on the figure the value of the
appropriated cross section is equal to 3.0 mb/sr.
